user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Litware Inc.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-20 10:09 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Litware Inc whitespace analysis

Just checking in on the whitespace analysis proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 15:00 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Litware Inc

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about whitespace analysis. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-14 13:06 EST
Subject: Re: whitespace analysis Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the whitespace analysis proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-02-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft), David (Litware Inc)

[00:02:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on whitespace analysis from our last session.

[00:06:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] David (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:14:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:18:00] David (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:23:00] David (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:28:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:31:00] David (Litware Inc): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:35:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:38:00] David (Litware Inc): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Litware Inc: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-S trap (Separation of pre-pack from portfolio-planning means CSA gets pre-packs for meet); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-S failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-S failure mode (Separation of pre-pack from portfolio-planning means CSA gets pre-packs for meet)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF32-S -- Separation of pre-pack from portfolio-planning means CSA gets pre-packs for meet
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
